foundations-score-and-ddpm.claim_text: Score-based generative modelling and denoising diffusion probabilistic models (NCSN, DDPM) establish a learnable noise-prediction objective whose reverse process samples high-quality data from a tractable Gaussian prior, giving the field its core modelling vocabulary.

candidates.paper_title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Ho et al. DDPM paper directly introduces the noise-prediction epsilon-objective and beats GAN-class samplers on CIFAR-10 and LSUN unconditional synthesis.

immediate_benefit.rationale: The simple epsilon-prediction training loop and reverse-process sampler are still the canonical recipe adopted by virtually every downstream diffusion implementation.

plausible.rationale: Derives the simplified variational bound from first principles and shows tight empirical agreement between predicted noise and high-quality samples.

scalable.rationale: Architecture is a standard UNet trained at image resolution; scaling beyond CIFAR/LSUN was demonstrated only by follow-up work.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports FID/IS on held-out splits and likelihoods, the protocol that became the de facto evaluation standard for diffusion samplers.

candidates.paper_title: Hierarchical Text-Conditional Image Generation with CLIP Latents

useful_outcomes.rationale: DALLE-2/unCLIP uses a diffusion decoder but the paper's outcomes are CLIP-latent text-to-image quality, not establishing the score/DDPM vocabulary.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Diffusion prior and decoder recipes are usable today, but they import rather than define noise-prediction objectives.

plausible.rationale: Empirical text-to-image results and CLIP-similarity ablations are sound and reproducible at OpenAI scale.

scalable.rationale: Trained at large scale with cascaded super-resolution diffusion stages, demonstrating diffusion compatibility with web-scale data.

how_to_validate.rationale: Uses CLIP-similarity, FID, and human evaluation, transferable protocols but not focused on score-matching validation.

candidates.paper_title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates DDPM-style noise-prediction models surpass GAN FID on ImageNet, providing landmark empirical support for the score/DDPM vocabulary.

immediate_benefit.rationale: ADM architecture and classifier-guidance code are widely reused as a drop-in DDPM backbone for downstream researchers.

plausible.rationale: Extensive architectural ablations on ImageNet 64-512 anchor the empirical claim that better architectures improve noise-prediction training.

scalable.rationale: Trains at ImageNet-512 with class conditioning, showing the DDPM recipe scales with compute and parameters.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID/sFID/IS on held-out ImageNet splits with rigorous ablation tables establish a reusable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: RePaint: Inpainting using Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
candidates.paper_journal: Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition

useful_outcomes.rationale: Applies an unmodified pretrained DDPM to free-form inpainting; demonstrates utility of the DDPM vocabulary but does not establish it.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Resampling scheme is plug-and-play for any DDPM, immediately adoptable but specific to inpainting.

plausible.rationale: Compares against autoregressive and GAN baselines across LSUN/CelebA mask types with clear quantitative gains.

scalable.rationale: Inherits DDPM's slow sampling and reverse-time resampling, limiting throughput on high-resolution inputs.

how_to_validate.rationale: Uses LPIPS and user studies on held-out masks; protocol is inpainting-specific, not generic to score matching.

candidates.paper_title: Score-Based Generative Modeling through Stochastic Differential Equations

useful_outcomes.rationale: Unifies NCSN score-matching with DDPM via SDEs and demonstrates state-of-the-art FID on CIFAR-10, directly supporting the score/DDPM vocabulary claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides reference code, variance-preserving and variance-exploding SDEs, and the probability-flow ODE that practitioners adopt directly.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous derivation from Anderson's reverse-time SDE backed by strong empirical CIFAR-10/CelebA-HQ results.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on CIFAR-10 and CelebA-HQ 256; ODE samplers make scaling tractable but not at billion-parameter scale here.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID, bits/dim on held-out splits, plus likelihood evaluation via the probability-flow ODE provide a comprehensive validation toolkit.

candidates.paper_title: Generative Modeling by Estimating Gradients of the Data Distribution

useful_outcomes.rationale: Song and Ermon's NCSN introduces multi-scale noise-perturbed score matching with annealed Langevin sampling, the foundation half of the NCSN/DDPM vocabulary.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Multi-noise score-matching loss and annealed Langevin sampler are reused across modern score-based diffusion implementations.

plausible.rationale: Manifold/low-density failure analysis motivates the design and is supported by CIFAR-10/CelebA empirical gains.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on 32x32 and 64x64 images; later SDE/DDPM work showed scaling beyond these resolutions.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID/IS on held-out CIFAR-10 with inception comparisons forms an early but transferable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Improved Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Shows that learned reverse variances and cosine schedules give DDPMs competitive log-likelihoods, refining the core noise-prediction objective.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Cosine schedule and hybrid loss are immediately adopted recipes in nearly every modern DDPM training pipeline.

plausible.rationale: Ablations on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet-64 directly attribute improvements to specific schedule and variance modifications.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrates 1000x sampling speedups via strided sampling, materially improving DDPM scalability.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports NLL/FID on held-out ImageNet-64 and CIFAR-10, a reusable likelihood-plus-FID validation pattern.

candidates.paper_title: Elucidating the Design Space of Diffusion-Based Generative Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: EDM's unified preconditioning, sampling, and training design clarifies the noise-prediction objective and sets CIFAR-10 FID records.

immediate_benefit.rationale: EDM preconditioning and Heun sampler are widely adopted as the modern reference recipe for score/DDPM training.

plausible.rationale: Systematic decoupling of design choices is supported by extensive controlled ablations on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet.

scalable.rationale: Sampler reduces NFEs by 5x, enabling efficient large-scale training; demonstrated on ImageNet-64 but not billion-parameter.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID, NFE budget, and exhaustive ablation tables on held-out splits make the validation protocol exceptionally transferable.

candidates.paper_title: Variational Diffusion Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: VDM achieves state-of-the-art image likelihoods by jointly learning the noise schedule, sharpening the noise-prediction objective's likelihood interpretation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Learnable-SNR formulation and continuous-time VLB are usable today as a drop-in replacement for fixed-schedule DDPM training.

plausible.rationale: Derives the loss as a single-variable SNR integral and confirms with strong CIFAR-10/ImageNet density estimation results.

scalable.rationale: Continuous-time formulation simplifies scaling and trains on ImageNet-64 likelihoods at the time.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports bits/dim and FID on held-out CIFAR-10/ImageNet-64, a standard transferable likelihood-and-quality protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Consistency Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Consistency models distill or train directly to one-step samplers, building on but extending beyond the classical noise-prediction vocabulary.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source one-step sampler is adopted in fast diffusion inference pipelines for both image and beyond.

plausible.rationale: Distillation and isolated-training results on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet-64 support the consistency-trajectory claim.

scalable.rationale: One-step generation dramatically lowers inference cost, with demonstrations at ImageNet-64 and 256.

how_to_validate.rationale: Uses FID with NFE breakdowns on held-out ImageNet-64/256, a clean and transferable few-step evaluation protocol.

foundations-continuous-time-sde.claim_text: Continuous-time score-based stochastic differential equations unify discrete-time DDPM with score-matching and make implicit, deterministic, and accelerated samplers (DDIM, probability-flow ODE) natural special cases.

candidates.paper_title: Score-Based Generative Modeling through Stochastic Differential Equations

useful_outcomes.rationale: Song et al. SDE paper is the canonical statement of the claim: VP/VE SDEs unify DDPM and NCSN and yield the probability-flow ODE.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Reference VP/VE SDE code and probability-flow ODE sampler are directly adopted by virtually every continuous-time diffusion work today.

plausible.rationale: Derivation from Anderson's reverse SDE is mathematically rigorous and matched by strong CIFAR-10 FID/likelihood results.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrates CelebA-HQ 1024 generation, showing the SDE framework scales to high-resolution generation.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID, bits/dim, and ODE-based exact likelihood on held-out splits provide a near-complete transferable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Elucidating the Design Space of Diffusion-Based Generative Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: EDM frames training and sampling explicitly as a continuous-time ODE/SDE and derives Heun-based deterministic samplers as natural special cases.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Heun deterministic sampler and EDM preconditioning are widely adopted as the default continuous-time recipe today.

plausible.rationale: Controlled ablations rigorously isolate the effect of each continuous-time design knob on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet FID.

scalable.rationale: Reduces NFE by 5x and demonstrates ImageNet-64 SOTA, supporting practical large-scale continuous-time sampling.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID-vs-NFE curves on held-out splits offer a transferable validation protocol for SDE/ODE samplers.

candidates.paper_title: SDEdit: Guided Image Synthesis and Editing with Stochastic Differential Equations

useful_outcomes.rationale: Builds explicitly on the SDE framework for guided editing, using continuous-time reverse SDE simulation, but is application-focused rather than unifying.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Reverse-SDE editing recipe runs on any SDE-trained diffusion and is immediately adoptable for guided synthesis.

plausible.rationale: Quantitative and human-evaluation results on stroke-based editing benchmarks support the reverse-SDE editing claim.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated at moderate image resolutions; scaling depends on the underlying SDE diffusion backbone.

how_to_validate.rationale: Mixes user studies with FID/LPIPS; protocol partially transfers but is editing-specific.

candidates.paper_title: DPM-Solver: A Fast ODE Solver for Diffusion Probabilistic Model Sampling in Around 10 Steps

useful_outcomes.rationale: DPM-Solver derives high-order ODE solvers for the diffusion probability-flow ODE, exactly the accelerated-sampler special case the claim names.

immediate_benefit.rationale: DPM-Solver and DPM-Solver++ are the default fast samplers in Stable Diffusion and Diffusers; directly adoptable today.

plausible.rationale: Exponential-integrator analysis is mathematically derived and matched by 10-step FID parity with 1000-step DDPM.

scalable.rationale: Reduces sampling cost by 50-100x and is applied at Stable Diffusion scale in production.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID-vs-NFE on held-out CIFAR-10/CelebA/ImageNet benchmarks provides a transferable fast-sampler validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Pseudo Numerical Methods for Diffusion Models on Manifolds

useful_outcomes.rationale: PNDM derives pseudo numerical solvers for the diffusion ODE, directly an accelerated deterministic sampler special case.

immediate_benefit.rationale: PLMS sampler is shipped in HuggingFace Diffusers and immediately usable on pretrained DDPMs.

plausible.rationale: Linear multistep analysis and 20-step FID parity with 1000-step DDPM on CIFAR-10/CelebA support the claim.

scalable.rationale: Order-of-magnitude speedup applies to large pretrained models including Stable Diffusion.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports FID at varying NFE on held-out splits, a transferable accelerated-sampler validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: DDIM is exactly the implicit/deterministic accelerated sampler the claim names, derived from a non-Markovian forward process equivalent to the probability-flow ODE.

immediate_benefit.rationale: DDIM remains the most-used deterministic sampler; reusable on any pretrained DDPM without retraining.

plausible.rationale: Derivation from non-Markovian forward processes is rigorous and validated by 10-50x sampling speedups on CIFAR-10 and CelebA.

scalable.rationale: Inference-only modification scales trivially to any pretrained DDPM including Stable Diffusion-class models.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID-vs-step-count tables on held-out splits provide a transferable accelerated-sampler validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Flow Matching for Generative Modeling

useful_outcomes.rationale: Flow Matching trains CNFs with a regression objective that subsumes diffusion ODE training and includes score-SDE paths as a special case.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Flow-matching loss is directly adoptable as a continuous-time alternative to DDPM training and powers modern models like SD3 and Meta MovieGen.

plausible.rationale: Theoretically grounded conditional-flow construction is supported by ImageNet-64/128/256 likelihood and FID gains.

scalable.rationale: Simulation-free training scales to ImageNet-256 and underpins large production text-to-image and text-to-video systems.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out FID/NLL on ImageNet alongside ODE-likelihood evaluations form a transferable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Flow Straight and Fast: Learning to Generate and Transfer Data with Rectified Flow

useful_outcomes.rationale: Rectified flow learns straight ODE trajectories that connect to but extend the diffusion ODE family rather than directly unifying SDE and DDPM.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Rectified-flow training and reflow procedure are adopted in modern systems like SD3 and immediately usable.

plausible.rationale: Straight-trajectory results on CIFAR-10 and image-to-image transfer are well-supported empirically.

scalable.rationale: Reflow procedure enables few-step generation at modern scales, demonstrated in subsequent work.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID on held-out CIFAR-10 and transfer benchmarks provides a transferable ODE-sampler validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Diffusion Schrödinger Bridge with Applications to Score-Based Generative Modeling

useful_outcomes.rationale: Generalises score SDEs to Schrodinger Bridges with finite-time forward processes, extending rather than directly unifying DDPM and DDIM.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Iterative-proportional-fitting training is non-trivial to deploy compared to standard SDE/DDIM samplers.

plausible.rationale: Theoretical convergence guarantees and small-scale image/MNIST experiments support the bridge formulation.

scalable.rationale: IPF-based training has been demonstrated only at low resolutions and is computationally expensive to scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports FID on held-out MNIST/CelebA-class data, transferable but limited beyond the bridge setting.

candidates.paper_title: Sampling is as easy as learning the score: theory for diffusion models with minimal data assumptions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Provides convergence guarantees for SDE-based score generative models under minimal data assumptions, supporting but not constructing the unification.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Theoretical bounds inform sampler design but do not give immediately deployable code or recipe.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous probability theory establishing polynomial convergence with accurate score estimates is mathematically sound.

scalable.rationale: Analysis applies to arbitrary distributions but does not directly demonstrate empirical scaling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Theoretical paper without empirical FID/likelihood validation protocols.

foundations-latent-and-guidance.claim_text: Latent-space diffusion (LDM / Stable Diffusion) and classifier-free guidance are the two engineering innovations that made diffusion practical at modern scale — by moving the diffusion process into a perceptual / semantic latent and by enabling strong conditional generation without an explicit classifier.

candidates.paper_title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
candidates.paper_journal: Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition

useful_outcomes.rationale: LDM/Stable Diffusion directly introduces perceptual latent diffusion and demonstrates state-of-the-art high-resolution synthesis with order-of-magnitude compute reduction.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source Stable Diffusion checkpoints and cross-attention conditioning are the de facto practical recipe used worldwide today.

plausible.rationale: Two-stage VAE+diffusion design is rigorously ablated on LAION/ImageNet/COCO with strong FID and human-eval results.

scalable.rationale: Scales from 256x256 to 1024x1024 on LAION-5B and is the dominant production text-to-image backbone.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports FID/IS on held-out COCO/ImageNet plus class-conditional and text-conditional ablations, a transferable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Adding Conditional Control to Text-to-Image Diffusion Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: ControlNet adds spatial conditioning to Stable Diffusion but does not establish latent-diffusion or classifier-free guidance themselves.

immediate_benefit.rationale: ControlNet weights are widely deployed for pose/edge/depth control on top of Stable Diffusion, immediately usable.

plausible.rationale: Zero-convolution finetuning is supported by extensive qualitative and quantitative comparisons across many control modalities.

scalable.rationale: Trains on top of billion-parameter Stable Diffusion and runs in production pipelines.

how_to_validate.rationale: Uses FID, control-fidelity metrics, and user studies on diverse held-out conditions, partially transferable.

candidates.paper_title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis

useful_outcomes.rationale: Introduces classifier guidance, the precursor to classifier-free guidance, and demonstrates conditional fidelity-diversity tradeoffs on ImageNet.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Classifier-guidance code is widely reused and motivates the classifier-free formulation used at modern scale.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous ImageNet ablations directly demonstrate the guidance-vs-FID/IS tradeoff.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated at ImageNet-512 with class conditioning, scaling the guidance approach to high resolution.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID/sFID/IS on held-out ImageNet with guidance-strength sweeps form a reusable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Photorealistic Text-to-Image Diffusion Models with Deep Language Understanding

useful_outcomes.rationale: Imagen demonstrates classifier-free guidance at extreme scale with frozen T5 conditioning, directly supporting the guidance half of the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Imagen is not open-source, limiting direct adoption, though its dynamic-thresholding recipe is widely reused.

plausible.rationale: DrawBench and COCO-FID results plus T5 ablations rigorously support the role of frozen LM and classifier-free guidance.

scalable.rationale: Cascaded diffusion to 1024x1024 with billion-parameter T5 demonstrates production-scale viability.

how_to_validate.rationale: Introduces DrawBench and combines COCO-FID with human side-by-side preferences, a strong transferable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: LAION-5B: An open large-scale dataset for training next generation image-text models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Dataset paper enabling latent-diffusion training at scale but not itself a latent-diffusion or guidance contribution.

immediate_benefit.rationale: LAION-5B is the dataset that practitioners directly download to train modern open latent-diffusion models.

plausible.rationale: Documents dataset construction, CLIP filtering, and validation experiments that justify its utility for image-text training.

scalable.rationale: 5.85 billion CLIP-filtered image-text pairs is the largest open dataset and underpins production diffusion models.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates by training CLIP and small diffusion models on subsets, partially transferable but not a guidance/latent protocol.

candidates.paper_title: GLIDE: Towards Photorealistic Image Generation and Editing with Text-Guided Diffusion Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: GLIDE directly compares CLIP guidance vs classifier-free guidance and finds classifier-free preferred, supporting the guidance half of the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Classifier-free guidance schedule from GLIDE is the recipe widely adopted in modern text-to-image diffusion pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Side-by-side human evaluation and FID/CLIP-score sweeps rigorously support the guidance comparison.

scalable.rationale: Trained at 3.5B parameters on large filtered datasets and released a smaller variant for community use.

how_to_validate.rationale: Combines COCO-FID with human preference studies under both guidance regimes, a reusable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Classifier-Free Diffusion Guidance

useful_outcomes.rationale: Ho and Salimans introduce classifier-free guidance directly, eliminating the auxiliary classifier and matching the second half of the claim verbatim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Joint conditional+unconditional training with guided sampling is the default conditional-generation recipe in every modern diffusion pipeline.

plausible.rationale: Class-conditional ImageNet ablations directly demonstrate FID/IS tradeoffs equivalent to classifier guidance.

scalable.rationale: Trivially scales because no extra classifier network is needed; used in Stable Diffusion, Imagen, DALLE.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports FID and IS on held-out ImageNet under guidance-strength sweeps, a transferable conditional-generation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Cascaded Diffusion Models for High Fidelity Image Generation

useful_outcomes.rationale: Cascaded super-resolution diffusion is a complementary scaling strategy, not latent diffusion or classifier-free guidance per se.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Cascaded super-resolution recipe is used in Imagen and DALLE-2 but not the direct latent/guidance innovation.

plausible.rationale: ImageNet class-conditional FID/IS results support the cascade-with-noise-conditioning-augmentation claim.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated up to ImageNet-256 with multi-stage pipelines, scalable but more complex than latent diffusion.

how_to_validate.rationale: FID/IS on held-out ImageNet with noise-augmentation ablations, transferable but not focused on latent/guidance.

candidates.paper_title: Imagen Video: High Definition Video Generation with Diffusion Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Applies cascaded video diffusion with classifier-free guidance to text-to-video but does not advance latent diffusion or introduce new guidance methods.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Imagen Video is closed-source and the pipeline is video-specific, limiting direct researcher adoption.

plausible.rationale: Per-frame and temporal-FID/CLIP-score results plus user studies support video-quality claims.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrates 1280x768 24fps generation at billion-parameter cascaded scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Mix of video-FVD, CLIP-score, and human evaluation on held-out prompts; transferable to video but less to image latent/guidance work.

candidates.paper_title: SDXL: Improving Latent Diffusion Models for High-Resolution Image Synthesis

useful_outcomes.rationale: SDXL is a direct scaling of the LDM/Stable Diffusion latent-diffusion recipe with new conditioning schemes and a refiner stage.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source SDXL checkpoints with classifier-free guidance are immediately usable by the open community.

plausible.rationale: Reports human preference and COCO-FID gains over SD1.5/SD2 with controlled conditioning ablations.

scalable.rationale: 3x larger UNet and dual text encoders trained on multi-aspect-ratio data demonstrate scaling of latent diffusion.

how_to_validate.rationale: Human preference studies and COCO-FID under classifier-free guidance, a reusable validation protocol.
